# protocols-v2_default_ospf_interface (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `metadata` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--metadata))
- `spec` (Attributes) DefaultOSPFInterfaceSpec defines the desired state of DefaultOSPFInterface (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--spec))

### Optional

- `alarms` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--alarms))
- `api_version` (String)
- `deviations` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--deviations))
- `kind` (String)
- `name` (String) name of the DefaultOSPFInterface
- `namespace` (String) the namespace scope in which to operate
- `status` (Attributes) DefaultOSPFInterfaceStatus defines the observed state of DefaultOSPFInterface (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--status))

<a id="nestedatt--metadata"></a>
### Nested Schema for `metadata`

Required:

- `name` (String)
- `namespace` (String)

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String)
- `labels` (Map of String)


<a id="nestedatt--spec"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec`

Required:

- `default_ospf_area` (String) Reference to a DefaultOSPFArea.
- `default_ospf_instance` (String) Reference to a Default OSPF Instance on which the Default OSPF area is configured.
- `interface` (String) Reference to either a DefaultInterface or SystemInterface.

Optional:

- `bfd` (Attributes) Configure BFD on the OSPF interface. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--spec--bfd))
- `dead_interval_seconds` (Number) Dead Interval in seconds.
- `hello_interval_seconds` (Number) Hello Interval in seconds.
- `interface_kind` (String) Reference to the Kind of interface to enable OSPF on.
- `metric` (Number) Interface metric.
- `mtu` (Number) OSPF interface MTU
- `passive` (Boolean) Configure the OSPF interface as passive.
- `type` (String) OSPF interface type.

<a id="nestedatt--spec--bfd"></a>
### Nested Schema for `spec.bfd`

Optional:

- `enabled` (Boolean) Enables BFD on the OSPF interface.
- `strict_mode` (Boolean) Enables BFD Strict Mode on the OSPF interface.
- `strict_mode_hold_down_seconds` (Number) Enables Hold Down Timer for BFD Strict Mode, in seconds.



<a id="nestedatt--alarms"></a>
### Nested Schema for `alarms`

Optional:

- `critical` (Number)
- `major` (Number)
- `minor` (Number)
- `warning` (Number)


<a id="nestedatt--deviations"></a>
### Nested Schema for `deviations`

Optional:

- `count` (Number)


<a id="nestedatt--status"></a>
### Nested Schema for `status`

Optional:

- `health` (Number) Indicates the health score of the OSPF interface.
- `health_score_reason` (String) Indicates the reason for the health score.
- `last_change_time` (String) The time when the state of the resource last changed.
- `neighbors` (Attributes List) Neighbor status within the OSPF interface. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--status--neighbors))
- `num_neighbors` (Number) The number of discovered neighbors.
- `operational_state` (String) Operational state of the OSPF interface.

<a id="nestedatt--status--neighbors"></a>
### Nested Schema for `status.neighbors`

Optional:

- `adjacency_state` (String) Adjacency state of the OSPF neighbor.
- `neighbor_router_id` (String) Router ID of the OSPF neighbor.
